message:
fix(install.sh): probe /dev/tty by opening it, not just testing -r

In some CI harnesses (e.g. the Dicklesworthstone agent swarm) [[ -r /dev/tty ]]
returns true but reading from /dev/tty fails with 'No such device or address',
which makes the curl|bash self-reexec path fail just before handing control
off to the real installer body.  Probing with '( : </dev/tty ) 2>/dev/null'
actually attempts to open the device, so we only route stdin there when it
will work.  Falls back to </dev/null otherwise, which makes later read -r
fail fast (the correct behavior under non-interactive curl|bash).

Follow-up to 5808227 (issue #250).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -82,10 +82,11 @@ if [[ -z "${BR_INSTALLER_SELF_REEXEC:-}" ]] \
     if [[ "$__br_self_fetched" -eq 1 ]] && [[ -s "$__br_self_tmp" ]]; then
         chmod 0700 "$__br_self_tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
         export BR_INSTALLER_SELF_REEXEC=1
-        # Route interactive input to the controlling tty if one exists so
-        # `read -r ans` prompts work; otherwise close stdin so reads fail
-        # fast instead of eating script bytes.
-        if [[ -r /dev/tty ]]; then
+        # Route interactive input to the controlling tty if one is usable.
+        # `[[ -r /dev/tty ]]` returns true in some CI harnesses where
+        # opening /dev/tty actually fails with "No such device or address",
+        # so probe by opening it in a subshell first.
+        if ( : </dev/tty ) 2>/dev/null; then
             exec bash "$__br_self_tmp" "$@" </dev/tty
         else
             exec bash "$__br_self_tmp" "$@" </dev/null
